weather_report:
Subiaco wakes under a pewter sky, the light gentled and cool, the air smelling faintly of wet stone. Clouds are thickening and, toward late morning and early afternoon, light rain looks set to drift through in soft sheets. Sunshine will be scarce today, more a pale suggestion behind the overcast, and while the cloud keeps the glare down, midday UV can still nibble at the edges, so a dab of sunscreen won’t go astray. The day feels calm rather than bleak: clear enough between showers to see the day get on with itself, streets rinsed, leaves lacquered, everything a notch quieter.

Dress for a mild, damp winter turn: a light waterproof jacket, layers you can peel, closed shoes that don’t mind a puddle, and a small umbrella for the fickle bursts. It’s a good time to run a short errand on foot before the rain settles in, linger over a late-morning coffee by a window, or take a brief stroll through a park when the drizzle slackens, listening to the softened world. If you’re staying in, open a window just enough for the fresh, rain-bright air and tend to something unhurried—sorting a shelf, sketching, writing a note you’ve meant to send—while the day hums along in a gentle grey.
